title: J-ART: A Japanese Adversarial Red-Team Framework for Application-Layer LLM Security and Cost-Efficiency
description:
J-ART is an open, reproducible harness that evaluates the application-structure layer of LLM deployments (model x system-prompt strength x guardrail x retrieval), not bare models. It applies Japanese-specific obfuscation transforms (gyaru-moji, vertical/newline splitting, keigo framing, double-talk, Base64, leet/zero-width smuggling) that defeat naive keyword filters, grounds attacks in MITRE ATLAS (10 inference-time techniques), compares five guardrail settings (none / keyword / normalizing-regex / LLM / Llama Guard), and reports a defense rate with Wilson 95% confidence intervals alongside a cost-efficiency metric. Attacks use harmless proxy markers (a fictitious canary and benign output markers); no weaponizable content is included, and the malicious instruction core is masked in all published artifacts.

This record archives the code, configuration, the technical report (paper/technical-report.md), and the deployment workflow.
